## ScispaceLaunches

SciSpace BioMed Agent [...] 4.4

Based on 19 reviews

Review Scispace?

Reviewers mostly see SciSpace as a practical way to get through dense papers faster, especially for literature reviews, unfamiliar fields, and reading across languages. They repeatedly praise plain-language explanations, quick Q&A, paper search, and draft-generation help. But the limits are also clear: heavy users say credits run out too quickly, some answers oversimplify or misread niche terms, and Copilot suggestions or Zotero syncing could be better. One clinical-research reviewer reports far more serious problems, including incorrect or fabricated references and poor support.

@brka This product reminds me of @Scispace's BioMed Agent. Are you heading to similar direction?

Marko Brkic [...] Marko Brkic

@rohanrecommends Our products share similarities, since both are meant to advance scientific research. But we focus mainly on "d'eep research": long runs that dive deep into specific topics the way scientists would. We also put heavy emphasis on data analysis, which is where we fine-tuned most and benchmarked - it's probably the best AI data scientist available

Anton Ponikarovskii

Using AI for scientific purposes is exactly what it was meant for, I think. Great launch, guys, congrats!

#### What's great

I work in clinical research and used SciSpace (PubGenius Inc.), which promotes itself as an AI assistant for improving scientific manuscripts, particularly in grammar and accurate bibliography generation. On February 9, 2026, I submitted a manuscript for review and was required to purchase an annual subscription to access the generated files.
